images.caption:
Figure 1:Overview of GraphRAG with the proposedAdaptive-masking for Graph Embedding(AGE) embedding.
1) Retrieval: Find graph elements relevant to the query using a non-parametric process.
2) Subgraph Construction: Extend retrieved graph elements with their adjacencies[G-Retriever].
3) Embedding: Use tokenizer and text embedder for textualized graph and query. Apply AGE for structured relationships of the graph.
4) Inference: Input embeddings into LLM to generate an answer.

Figure 2:Architecture for Adaptive-masking for Graph Embedding: During training,𝒉target{\bm{h}}_{\text{target}}is connected to the downstream for the target encoder training, while𝒉out{\bm{h}}_{\text{out}}is used during inference. The node sampler explores the optimal distribution for mask-based SSL for graphs. The loss functions train distinct sets of modules without overlap.

Figure 5:Node embedding of G-Retriever and AGE G-Retriever with sampling rateρ=0.3\rho=0.3using t-SNE[vanDerMaaten2008]: Nodes are colored by clustering node’s text (left two), or target error (the rightest).

Figure H.6:Investigation of core component arrangement: We tested our JEPA[LeCun2022APT]architecture with three different GNN arrangements, including (a) graph encoder only, (b) graph-structure-based aggregator only, and (c) both of them.
Figure H.7:Adaptive-masking for Graph Embedding in Generation Architecture: The node embedding module is trained on both prompt tuning loss and target loss.
Figure H.11:Training loss on Explain Graphs.

Figure H.13:The landscape of existing KGQA methods. GNN-based methods reason on dense subgraphs as they can handle complex and graph information. LLM-based methods employ the same LLM for both retrieval and reasoning due to its ability to understand natural language.
Figure H.14:Failure case visualization of AGE on WebQSP dataset.
Figure H.15:An example visualization of AGE on the ExplaGraphs dataset.
Figure H.16:Another example visualization of AGE on the ExplaGraphs dataset.
Figure H.17:An example visualization of AGE on the WebQSP dataset.
